div1.entryFree:
Discern, 1) to distinguish: I could d. no part of his face from the window, H4B II, 2, 86. an eye --ing thine honour from thy suffering, Lr. IV, 2, 52.
2) to see, to perceive, to distinguish by the eye; a) absol.: as far as I could well d. for smoke, H6A II, 2, 26. as I d., it burneth in the Capels' monument, Rom. V, 3, 126. b) trans.: Lucr. 619. Wint. III, 3, 138. H6A III, 2, 24. Lr. II, 4, 151. Oth. II, 1, 1. III, 3, 102. Cymb. I, 6, 84. Per. V, 1, 116.
